req.title: Account-level transaction limits (boundary variant)
req.statement: A customer shall configure daily per-channel limits within regulatory caps; changes take effect after a 2-hour cool-down. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-042 governs three distinct behaviours: (1) valid mid-range limit submission yields a 202 with pending status, (2) a limit exceeding the regulatory cap yields 422, and (3) boundary values at exactly the minimum and maximum thresholds are accepted or rejected per the spec. A fifth test covers the cool-down state transition (pending → active after 2 h). Together these five tests achieve EP, NEG, BVA (min-1, min, max, max+1), and STC coverage. Each test is self-contained via per-test account creation through <create_account> and <auth_headers> fixtures, ensuring idempotency.

tests.name: test_set_valid_mid_range_limit_returns_202_pending
preconditions: Account exists with no pending limit changes
Chosen limit value is strictly between regulatory min and max
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains effective_after timestamp approximately 2 hours from now
trace: R-FIN-042: valid limit within regulatory caps must be accepted
R-FIN-042: changes take effect after 2-hour cool-down
tests.executable:
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_set_valid_mid_range_limit_returns_202_pending(
    auth_headers, create_account, api_client
):
    account_id = create_account()
    payload = {"daily_online_limit": 5000}

    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "pending"

    effective_after = datetime.fromisoformat(body["effective_after"])
    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
    delta = effective_after - now
    assert timedelta(hours=1, minutes=55) <= delta <= timedelta(hours=2, minutes=5)

tests.name: test_set_limit_above_regulatory_cap_returns_422
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains error code referencing regulatory cap violation
trace: R-FIN-042: error pathway - 422 when over regulatory cap
tests.executable:
import pytest
from tests.constants import REGULATORY_CAP

def test_set_limit_above_regulatory_cap_returns_422(
    auth_headers, create_account, api_client
):
    account_id = create_account()
    over_cap = REGULATORY_CAP + 1
    payload = {"daily_online_limit": over_cap}

    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "regulatory_cap" in body.get("error_code", "").lower() or \
           "regulatory" in body.get("detail", "").lower()

tests.name: test_limit_transitions_to_active_after_cooldown
preconditions: Account exists with a pending limit change
Test environment supports time-travel or cool-down override via <advance_time>
actions: PATCH /accounts/{id}/limits with daily_online_limit=3000 to create pending state
Advance server clock past 2-hour cool-down via <advance_time(hours=2)>
GET /accounts/{id}/limits to read current effective limit
trace: R-FIN-042: changes take effect after 2-hour cool-down
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_limit_transitions_to_active_after_cooldown(
    auth_headers, create_account, api_client, advance_time
):
    account_id = create_account()
    new_limit = 3000

    patch_resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json={"daily_online_limit": new_limit},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert patch_resp.status_code == 202
    assert patch_resp.json()["status"] == "pending"

    advance_time(hours=2, account_id=account_id)

    get_resp = api_client.get(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    body = get_resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "active"
    assert body["daily_online_limit"] == new_limit

tests.name: test_set_limit_below_regulatory_min_returns_422
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body references minimum limit violation
trace: R-FIN-042: error pathway - 422 when below regulatory min
R-FIN-042: boundary behaviour at min-1 must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
from tests.constants import REGULATORY_MIN

def test_set_limit_below_regulatory_min_returns_422(
    auth_headers, create_account, api_client
):
    account_id = create_account()
    below_min = REGULATORY_MIN - 1
    payload = {"daily_online_limit": below_min}

    resp = api_client.patch(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/limits",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "minimum" in body.get("detail", "").lower() or \
           "min" in body.get("error_code", "").lower()
